content.text:
<user_query>
Quickly review remaining high-severity parity risks after latest edits in: frontend/src/config/routeAccessConfig.js, frontend/src/lib/tierResolver.js, frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.js, frontend/src/components/DashboardLayout.js, backend/tier_resolver.py, backend/routes/deps.py, frontend/src/config/pricingTiers.js, frontend/src/config/tiers.js. Return only HIGH issues if any; otherwise say none and list top 2 medium residuals.
</user_query>
Reading the listed files to compare frontend and backend tier/route parity.
Checking App routes and diffing FE vs BE route maps for gaps.

Here‚Äôs the assessment.

## HIGH

1. **Waitlist pages vs `minTier: 'starter'` (FE)**  
   In `routeAccessConfig.js`, waitlist routes use `minTier: 'starter'`, so `checkRouteAccess` / `canAccess` (via `tiers.js` ‚Üí `tierResolver`) treats **paid Foundation users as allowed**.  
   In `App.js`, `LaunchRoute` does `if (effectiveAccess === 'waitlist' && !privileged) { ‚Ä¶ Navigate to /more-features }`, so **every non‚Äìmaster user is redirected**, including paying customers.  
   On the backend, `tier_resolver.py` only encodes tier (`'starter'` for those paths)‚Äîthere is no waitlist dimension‚Äîso **API route checks align with ‚Äústarter allowed,‚Äù not with the LaunchRoute behavior.**  
   Net: nav/unlock logic, page gate, and BE tier checks disagree; paid users can see ‚Äúunlocked‚Äù nav but get bounced (and could still hit starter-gated APIs). That‚Äôs a **high-severity internal entitlement / parity break**.

2. **Master email on admin UI vs `get_admin_user` (FE vs BE)**  
   `ProtectedRoute` with `adminOnly` sets admin if `/auth/me` role is admin-class **or** `isPrivilegedUser(user)` (env master email).  
   `backend/routes/deps.py` `get_admin_user` only allows roles `admin`, `superadmin`, `super_admin`‚Äî**no `MASTER_ADMIN_EMAIL` bypass** (that exists on `get_super_admin`, not on `get_admin_user`).  
   If the master account‚Äôs JWT/DB role is not one of those, **UI can show admin routes while admin-scoped APIs return 403**‚Äîa **high-severity FE/BE parity and ops risk**.

---

If you treat (1) as ‚Äúby design‚Äù (waitlist = product flag) and (2) as ‚Äúmaster always has admin role in DB,‚Äù you could downgrade‚Äîbut as written in these files, both are real **HIGH** parity risks.

---

**If you instead want the alternate branch (‚Äúnone‚Äù on HIGH):** top **medium** residuals would be: **(a)** two hand-maintained route maps (`routeAccessConfig.js` vs `ROUTE_ACCESS` in `tier_resolver.py`) plus separate `API_ACCESS`, so drift stays likely; **(b)** `LaunchRoute` uses the string `paid` while the map uses `launchType: 'foundation'`, which is easy to misread when extending gates (behavior still works when `access="paid"` is passed explicitly).
